summary:
Ibuprofen has a theoretic potential to increase bleeding risk via inhibition of platelet function, yet available evidence does not show an association between its use and increased perioperative blood loss in hip arthroplasty. Clinical practices widely adopt ibuprofen for post-operative pain control, suggesting it is safely used without significant adverse effects on hemostasis.  

$\boxed{\text{C}}$

$\boxed{\text{C}}$. Answer choice: C
